2604.18266.title: Enhancing Tabular Anomaly Detection via Pseudo-Label-Guided Generation
2604.18266.paper_summary: Identifying anomalous instances in tabular data is essential for improving data reliability and maintaining system stability. Due to the scarcity of ground-truth anomaly labels, existing methods mainly rely on unsupervised anomaly detection models, or exploit a small number of labeled anomalies to facilitate detection via sample generation or contrastive learning. However, unsupervised methods lack sufficient anomaly awareness, while current generation and contrastive approaches tend to compute anomalies globally, overlooking the localized anomaly patterns of tabular features, resulting in suboptimal detection performance. To address these limitations, we propose PLAG, a pseudo-label-guided anomaly generation method designed to enhance tabular anomaly detection. Specifically, by utilizing pseudo-anomalies as guidance signals and decoupling the overall anomaly quantification of a sample into an accumulation of feature-level abnormalities, PLAG not only effectively obviates the need for scarce ground-truth labels but also provides a novel perspective for the model to comprehend localized anomalous signals at a fine-grained level. Furthermore, a two-stage data selection strategy is proposed, integrating format verification and uncertainty estimation to rigorously filter candidate samples, thereby ensuring the fidelity and diversity of the synthetic anomalies. Ultimately, these filtered synthetic anomalies serve as robust discriminative guidance, empowering the model to better separate normal and anomalous instances. Extensive experiments demonstrate that PLAG achieves state-of-the-art performance against eight representative baselines. Moreover, as a flexible framework, it integrates seamlessly with existing unsupervised detectors, consistently boosting F1-scores by 0.08 to 0.21.
2604.18266.paper_summary_zh: 識別表格數據中的異常實例對於提高數據可靠性和維持系統穩定性至關重要。由於缺乏真實的異常標籤，現有的方法主要依賴無監督的異常檢測模型，或利用少量標記的異常來通過樣本生成或對比學習促進檢測。然而，無監督方法缺乏足夠的異常意識，而當前的生成和對比方法往往全局計算異常，忽略了表格特徵的局部異常模式，導致檢測性能不佳。為了解決這些限制，我們提出了PLAG，一種以偽標籤為指導的異常生成方法，旨在增強表格異常檢測。具體而言，通過利用偽異常作為指導信號，並將樣本的整體異常量化解耦為特徵級異常的累積，PLAG不僅有效地消除了對稀缺真實標籤的需求，還為模型提供了一種新視角，以理解細粒度的局部異常信號。此外，我們提出了一種兩階段數據選擇策略，整合格式驗證和不確定性估計，以嚴格篩選候選樣本，從而確保合成異常的真實性和多樣性。最終，這些篩選出的合成異常作為穩健的區分指導，賦予模型更好地分離正常和異常實例的能力。廣泛的實驗表明，PLAG在八個代表性基準上達到了最先進的性能。此外，作為一個靈活的框架，它與現有的無監督檢測器無縫集成，穩定地將F1分數提高0.08到0.21。
2604.18266.author: Wei Huang et.al.
2604.18266.authors: Wei Huang, Yuxuan Xiong, Hezhe Qiao, Yu-Ming Shang, Xiangling Fu, Guansong Pang
